name: Profiles Management
description: Adds multi-user profile switching to Jellyfin. A single account can have up to five isolated profiles with separate watch history, parental controls, and library access.

versions.changelog: Security: removed debug logging that wrote full Authorization header values (including live session tokens) to the Jellyfin log on every /list call. Tokens are no longer written to any log file. Also updated plugin description in the catalog.

Fix: Switch Profile button rendering as a full-height bar on some Jellyfin installations. Root cause: Jellyfin's React layout applies CSS transforms to body and inner containers, causing position:fixed to be contained by the transformed element instead of the viewport. Fix: fallback button is now appended to the html element (outside the transform chain) with all styles set inline. Also improved header container detection to use a parent-of-existing-button strategy, making it work across Jellyfin versions that use different class names.

Performance: reduce route-check interval to 150ms; add DOM-based video player detection so the Switch Profile button hides the instant the OSD appears (no URL change needed). UX: button now fades out smoothly instead of jumping. Fix: button no longer appears on any admin/dashboard page — hidden across all server-management routes except the Profiles plugin settings page. Fix: clicking the button now dims it immediately to confirm the tap registered. Fix: eliminated race condition where rapid React re-renders could destroy the button element mid-click.

Fix library access not persisting across server restarts or plugin updates. The plugin now stores enabled libraries in its own configuration (not just Jellyfin's user policy), and re-applies them on every profile switch. Existing profiles auto-migrate on first switch. Edit modal now correctly shows only the libraries the profile actually has access to.

Fix Switch Profile button: now hidden during playback and on the server dashboard. Restore visibility on item detail/browse pages. Add explicit Enter/Space keydown handler for TV D-pad compatibility. Expanded platform docs (Roku, Xbox, PS4/5, Electron, Android native, Tizen/webOS version matrices, tvOS).

Add per-profile inactivity lockout timer (configurable: 1m / 5m / 10m / 20m / 30m / 1h / never). Silent PIN auto-submit on correct keystroke. Security fix: remove auth token from debug log. Cross-platform improvements: pointer events for Magic Remote, auto-focus on profile overlay.
